title: Bootstrap: Nix devshell + build skeleton
description: Stand up the project skeleton so all subsequent work builds green. flake.nix Nix devshell with pinned toolchain (clang+gcc, C++20), CMake (or Meson) build, directory layout (include/shimmy/, bench/, tests/), HdrHistogram + a microbench dependency wired in, and a trivial stub that compiles and runs under 'nix develop'. This is the first claimed bead of the epic.
design: Nix flake for reproducible toolchain. CMake preferred unless Meson proves cleaner for header-only+bench layout. Layout: include/shimmy/ (header-only lib), bench/ (compiled, HdrHistogram, perf counters, CSV out), tests/ (correctness + TSan). Pin deps in flake for reproducible benchmark numbers — 'data doesn't lie' requires a fixed toolchain.
acceptance_criteria: 'nix develop' drops into a shell with C++20 clang+gcc, cmake, hdrhistogram available. A stub target compiles and runs. Directory layout (include/shimmy, bench, tests) exists. CI can invoke the build. Documented build/test commands land in CLAUDE.md (replacing the placeholder Build & Test section).
notes:
BOOTSTRAP COMPLETE — verified end-to-end under nix develop (clang+gcc+TSan).

BUILD SYSTEM: CMake (3.30.5 from nixpkgs) + Ninja. Library = INTERFACE (header-only) target shimmy::shimmy. tests/ and bench/ are the only compiled targets. Options: SHIMMY_BUILD_TESTS, SHIMMY_BUILD_BENCH, SHIMMY_ENABLE_TSAN.

TEST FRAMEWORK: GoogleTest (gtest 1.15.2 from nixpkgs). Chosen over Catch2 because it pairs with Google Benchmark (same ecosystem), has gtest_discover_tests CMake integration, and good death-test support for the concurrency/TSan work in shimmy-4d4.

PINNED nixpkgs REV: 50ab793786d9de88ee30ec4e4c24fb4236fc2674 (channel nixos-24.11), locked in flake.lock. Gives clang 18.1.8, gcc 13.3.0, cmake 3.30.5. DO NOT bump casually — toolchain change moves benchmark numbers.

GOTCHA — HdrHistogram is NOT in nixpkgs. Built as a pinned source derivation in flake.nix: HdrHistogram/HdrHistogram_c tag 0.11.8, hash sha256-TFlrC4bgK8o5KRZcLMlYU5EO9Oqaqe08PjJgmsUl51M=. Consumed via pkg-config IMPORTED_TARGET hdr_histogram (header <hdr/hdr_histogram.h>). To bump: change rev+hash in flake.nix.

GOTCHA — nix flakes only see git-TRACKED files. New files must be 'git add -N'd before nix develop / nix flake lock will see them (we did this; user still needs to git add+commit).

EXACT VERIFIED COMMANDS (all inside nix develop):
  cmake -S . -B build -G Ninja && cmake --build build
  ctest --test-dir build --output-on-failure
  ./build/bench/shimmy_bench
  gcc:  cmake -S . -B build-gcc -G Ninja -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=g++
  tsan: cmake -S . -B build-tsan -G Ninja -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++ -DSHIMMY_ENABLE_TSAN=ON -DSHIMMY_BUILD_BENCH=OFF

CI: .github/workflows/ci.yml — cachix/install-nix-action, clang+gcc matrix build/test/bench, separate TSan job.

FOR shimmy-4d4: header-only lib goes in include/shimmy/ (stub is version.hpp). Add real tests to tests/ (gtest), benchmarks to bench/. TSan build path already wired (SHIMMY_ENABLE_TSAN). Build did NOT exercise host toolchain for numbers — always build inside nix develop.
close_reason: Built reproducible bootstrap, verified end-to-end under 'nix develop'. DECISIONS: (1) CMake+Ninja over Meson — INTERFACE target cleanly models the header-only lib, gtest_discover_tests integrates testing. (2) GoogleTest over Catch2 — same ecosystem as Google Benchmark, strong CMake + death-test support for upcoming TSan stress. (3) nixpkgs pinned to 50ab793 (nixos-24.11) in flake.lock for reproducible numbers (clang 18.1.8, gcc 13.3.0, cmake 3.30.5). (4) HdrHistogram is NOT in nixpkgs, so pinned as a source derivation (HdrHistogram_c 0.11.8) in flake.nix rather than fetched at CMake time — keeps the histogram dep as reproducible as the rest. All 5 acceptance criteria met: nix develop gives clang+gcc+cmake+HdrHistogram; stub (version.hpp header + gtest test + bench linking gbench & HdrHistogram) compiles & runs under BOTH compilers and a TSan build; layout include/shimmy + bench + tests exists; CI workflow + one-liner provided; CLAUDE.md Build & Test section replaced with real commands. Tests 2/2 pass, bench emits timings. NOT committed/pushed per instructions — user will push.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Post-close fix (verified by main session): hdr_histogram.pc declares 'Requires: zlib' but zlib was absent from the devshell pkg-config path, emitting a configure-time warning. Harmless for the smoke bench (in-memory recording only) but would break linking the moment a bead uses HdrHistogram's file-logging/compression APIs. Fixed by adding pkgs.zlib to commonTools in flake.nix. Re-verified: 'pkg-config --exists zlib' true, configure clean, 2/2 tests pass. Note for shimmy-4d4/7d8: build inside 'nix develop'; IDE standalone-clang diagnostics (header-not-found) are false positives from missing -Iinclude, not real build errors.

title: Core SPMC ring buffer (header-only, in-process)
description: The heart of shimmy: a templated single-producer multi-consumer fixed-block ring buffer, header-only, validated in-process (no shmem dependency for the unit under test). Producer write + publish, consumer zero-copy read with lap detection, sequencing. Both overflow policies (Overwrite, Backpressure) and all wait strategies (Spin, SpinThenYield, Futex) as compile-time tag types.
design:
SLOT-PUBLISH PROTOCOL (the key decision): per-slot atomic<u64> seq stamp acts as a SEQLOCK version. A single trailing release stamp is INSUFFICIENT for the zero-copy Overwrite path — TSan-confirmed torn reads. Producer does TWO-PHASE publish: (1) store(writing_seq, release) BEFORE touching payload, (2) atomic_thread_fence(release), (3) plain stores of len+payload, (4) store(seq, release). writing_seq = ~0-1 sentinel; empty_seq = ~0. Consumer do_read: acquire-load stamp; ==want -> ok; ==writing_seq -> empty(retry); ==empty_seq or <want -> empty; >want -> lapped. validate(): atomic_thread_fence(acquire) THEN relaxed-load stamp, valid iff still ==read_seq_. The acquire FENCE (not an acquire load) is required so payload reads are ordered-before the re-check (acquire load only blocks later ops moving up, wrong direction for seqlock read side).

MEMORY ORDERS (all justified in-code): publish stamp store=release (min to make payload visible; relaxed broken, seq_cst unneeded — no cross-var total order). do_read stamp load=acquire (min; relaxed reads stale payload). validate=acquire FENCE + relaxed load (canonical seqlock read side). next_publish_=relaxed (producer-only; consumers sync on per-slot stamp not this). Backpressure consumer cursors: set_cursor=release / producer await=acquire (lossless handoff edge). register_consumer CAS=acq_rel. Futex: relaxed load to decide whether to sleep (kernel re-reads under futex lock; consumer re-validates with acquire after wake — futex wake is NOT a sync edge for payload).

FALSE-SHARING LAYOUT: next_publish_ on its own cache line; each consumer Cursor alignas(64); each Slot alignas(64). cache_line_size HARD-CODED to 64 (NOT std::hardware_destructive_interference_size) because it participates in the in-shmem byte layout and must NOT vary across gcc/clang/stdlib versions (gcc -Winterference-size ABI warning; libc++ historically missing it). x86-64 phase 1 => 64 is correct.

LAP DETECTION: stamp>want => producer lapped (slot holds want+k*Cap). resync(produced) jumps cursor to produced-Cap (oldest resident). Zero-copy validate catches mid-read lap (stamp changed to writing_seq or newer seq).

ZERO-COPY PAYLOAD RACE: under Overwrite the in-place payload access is the classic seqlock benign race (non-atomic producer write vs consumer read overlap). Correctness comes from validate() discarding torn views, NOT from atomic payload. Under Backpressure no race (slot never overwritten while held). Verified TSan-clean across millions of forced laps on a 2-slot ring; documented as benign race, do not "fix" with atomic payload without measuring.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Header-only, compiles with both gcc & clang C++20. (2) Single-producer/multi-consumer correctness test passes. (3) Overwrite: lapped consumer detects sequence gap, reports loss, resyncs — covered by a test. (4) Backpressure: producer waits for slowest consumer, lossless — covered by a test. (5) Zero-copy read + validate() lap-detection test passes. (6) Concurrency stress under TSan is clean. (7) All 3 wait strategies instantiate and pass a smoke test.
notes:
FILES: include/shimmy/ring.hpp (Ring<BlockSize,Capacity,Overflow,MaxConsumers,Wait> + Consumer<Ring>), include/shimmy/policies.hpp (Overwrite/Backpressure tags), include/shimmy/wait_strategy.hpp (Spin/SpinThenYield<N>/Futex tags), include/shimmy/detail/cache.hpp. Tests: tests/ring_basic_test.cpp, ring_lap_test.cpp, ring_concurrency_test.cpp (wired into shimmy_tests). Bench: bench/ring_bench.cpp (new exe shimmy_ring_bench; minimal hot-path only, full harness=shimmy-7d8).

FOR shimmy-52e (SHARED MEMORY) — DESIGN CONSTRAINTS ALREADY HONORED, MUST PRESERVE:
* Ring has NO internal pointers. Storage = inline std::array<Slot,Capacity>; consumers address slots by index (seq & mask). Consumer holds an integer cursor_id, not a back-pointer to ring state.
* Ring is std::is_standard_layout (asserted in test Layout.RingIsShmemPlaceable), no vtables/virtuals. Can be placement-new-d over an mmap region.
* Ring::init() reinitializes all stamps to empty_seq and zeroes cursors — call it after placement-new over fresh shmem. Has a trailing release fence so a freshly-initialized ring is safely visible.
* All cross-thread state = std::atomic over fixed-width ints => position-independent, identical bytes across processes mapping at different addresses.
* cache_line_size is hard-coded 64 so a gcc producer and clang consumer agree on byte layout. shimmy-52e MUST keep this constant (do not switch to hardware_destructive_interference_size).
* Consumer currently holds Ring* (in-process). shimmy-52e replaces that with an offset-based view but the PROTOCOL is identical — only the addressing changes.
* Backpressure consumer registry (inline cursors[MaxConsumers] with in_use flags) is the seed of the §9 discovery/handshake epic; crash-safety (dead consumer holding a cursor stalls producer forever) is NOT handled — that is deferred per DESIGN §9.

FOR shimmy-7d8 (BENCH HARNESS): hot path is allocation/syscall-free. Sanity floor measured in-process: BM_Publish64 ~1ns (1.02G items/s), BM_PublishReadValidate64 ~1.26ns. These are single-thread microbench floors WITHOUT the pinned-CPU/HdrHistogram rigor — treat as smoke, not headline numbers. Futex wait strategy: producer must call Wait::notify after publish (already wired via if constexpr Wait::blocks). Futex word = low 32 bits of per-slot stamp (asserts little-endian).

VERIFIED: clang 18 + gcc 13 builds clean (-Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wconversion -Wshadow), 18/18 ctest pass both. TSan (clang, SHIMMY_ENABLE_TSAN) 18/18, 0 warnings across 6 repeated runs + dedicated 2-slot maximal-lap zero-copy stress. All inside nix develop.
close_reason:
Implemented the core SPMC fixed-block ring header-only in include/shimmy/ (ring.hpp + policies.hpp + wait_strategy.hpp + detail/cache.hpp), validated in-process. All 7 acceptance criteria met and VERIFIED inside nix develop: (1) header-only, builds clean on clang-18 AND gcc-13 C++20 with -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wconversion -Wshadow; (2) SPMC correctness — multiple consumers each read the full ordered stream when keeping up; (3) Overwrite lapped consumer detects the sequence gap, reports loss, resyncs to produced-Cap and continues in order; (4) Backpressure is lossless — producer waits on inline producer-visible consumer cursors, every consumer reads all N msgs (total==N*consumers, 0 failures); (5) zero-copy read()+validate() lap detection works, including a deterministic mid-read-lap test and a threaded 2-slot maximal-lap stress; (6) TSan clean — 18/18 tests, 0 warnings across 6 repeated runs; (7) all 3 wait strategies (Spin, SpinThenYield<N>, Futex) instantiate and pass a smoke read.

KEY TECHNICAL DECISION: a single trailing release stamp was INSUFFICIENT (TSan exposed torn/non-monotonic zero-copy reads). Fixed with a two-phase seqlock publish: store(writing_seq, release) -> release fence -> plain payload stores -> store(seq, release); consumer validates with an acquire FENCE + relaxed reload (canonical seqlock read side). Every memory_order is justified in-code. Layout is pointer-free, standard-layout, no vtable, cache-line-isolated (hard-coded 64B), and init()-able over fresh memory — designed so shimmy-52e can place it in shared memory unchanged. Full protocol, ordering rationale, false-sharing layout, and shmem/bench hand-off recorded in --design/--notes. Not committed/pushed per instructions.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Post-close review (main session, independently re-verified clang/gcc/TSan all 18/18, 0 TSan warnings across repeated runs — agent report holds). Found one undocumented operational contract, now documented in ring.hpp register_consumer(): BACKPRESSURE requires all consumers to register BEFORE the producer publishes its Capacity-th message. A late-joining Backpressure consumer registers with cursor=0 but seq 0's slot may already be overwritten -> producer deadlocks in await_consumers_past() waiting on a cursor that can never advance, while the consumer gets 'lapped' (which Backpressure doesn't handle). The tests sidestep this with a start barrier; the contract was real but unwritten. Code is correct as tested; this is a precondition, not a bug. Overwrite is unaffected (late consumers resync). No behavior change made — comment-only.

title: Benchmark + analytics harness (data backbone)
description: The 'data doesn't lie' backbone, built alongside the core (not after). Compiled bench targets producing HdrHistogram latency distributions (p50..p99.99), throughput sweeps across message size and consumer count, perf-counter capture, CSV output, and a CI regression gate. This is what converts every design claim into measured data.
design: HdrHistogram for latency tails. Sweep dimensions: msg size {64B,256B,1KB,4KB}, consumer count, wait strategy, overflow policy. Capture perf counters (cycles, cache-misses, coherence) where feasible. Emit CSV; publish full curves not single numbers. CI gate compares against a committed baseline and fails on regression beyond a threshold. Pin toolchain (see bootstrap bead) so numbers are comparable across runs.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Latency bench emits HdrHistogram p50..p99.99 per wait strategy. (2) Throughput sweep emits CSV for 64B/256B/1KB/4KB. (3) Headline number confirms >=10M msgs/sec @64B single producer (or reports the measured figure + sets the gate from it). (4) Per-tier p99.9 published: Spin/SpinThenYield/Futex each benched against its own impl. (5) CI regression gate fails the build on a meaningful slowdown vs baseline. (6) Output is reproducible run-to-run on the pinned toolchain.

title: shimmy core: SPMC shared-memory messaging (phase 1)
description: Build the phase-1 core of shimmy: a single-host, SPMC, shared-memory messaging library in C++20. A 'channel' is a fixed-block-size ring buffer (block size chosen at compile time), single producer fanning out to many consumers, variable-but-bounded payloads, zero-copy reads with lap detection. Header-only library + compiled benchmark/analytics harness. Linux-only, x86-64, single NUMA node. See DESIGN.md for full rationale and decisions. Guiding principle: all performance claims must be backed by benchmark data — speculation is useless.
design: Fixed-size-slot ring (not bip buffer): block = [u32 len][payload][unused tail], variable payload within fixed block. Power-of-2 capacity -> index by mask. Compile-time axes: BlockSize (NTTP), Capacity, overflow policy (Overwrite|Backpressure tag), max-consumers, wait strategy (Spin|SpinThenYield|Futex). Zero-copy read returns span into shmem + validate() rechecks sequence for lap detection. Both overflow policies supported per-channel (doubles test surface, accepted). x86-64 TSO assumed on common path but atomics expressed via correct C++ memory_order. Full design narrative in DESIGN.md (committed to repo as the durable design doc alongside this epic).
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 complete when: (1) core SPMC ring implemented header-only with both overflow policies and all 3 wait strategies; (2) shared-memory segment lifecycle implemented (shm_open/mmap, versioned header, hugepage option); (3) benchmark+analytics harness produces HdrHistogram latency + throughput sweep CSV in CI with a regression gate; (4) measured throughput >=10M msgs/sec single-producer @64B; (5) p99.9 latency benched and published per wait-strategy tier (Spin<200ns, SpinThenYield<1us, Futex<10us as hypotheses to confirm); (6) full bench curve published for 64B/256B/1KB/4KB.

title: Shared-memory segment lifecycle
description: Place the core ring in a named POSIX shared-memory segment so it works across processes. shm_open/mmap, a versioned segment header (magic + layout version), alignment, optional hugepages (MAP_HUGETLB), and teardown/unlink ownership semantics.
design: Segment header carries magic, layout version, BlockSize, Capacity, overflow policy id, consumer-cursor region. mmap the ring + cursors. MAP_HUGETLB optional (measure tail impact — open question). Define who owns unlink (creator) and how a late opener validates layout compatibility against its compile-time instantiation. Mismatch must fail loudly, not corrupt.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Producer creates a named segment; a separate process opens it and reads messages. (2) Segment header versioned; opening with an incompatible layout fails with a clear error, never silent corruption. (3) Hugepage path works when available and is benchmarked with vs without. (4) Clean teardown/unlink; no leaked segments after a normal run. (5) Cross-process integration test passes.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: HEADS-UP from shimmy-4d4 review: the Backpressure registration contract (all consumers must register before producer reaches Capacity) becomes much harder cross-process. In shared memory you cannot rely on an in-process start barrier — a process can mmap and start consuming at any time. shimmy-52e must either (a) restrict to Overwrite for the first cross-process cut, or (b) define how a Backpressure consumer safely attaches mid-stream (likely: start its cursor at the producer's current published seq, not 0). The general dynamic-attach/handshake solution is deferred to shimmy-uud. Decide and document which stance shimmy-52e takes. See ring.hpp register_consumer() comment for the full hazard description.

title: shimmy: discovery, handshake & crash-safety (deferred)
description: Deferred future epic, NOT part of phase 1. Producer/consumer discovery and handshake (how a consumer finds a channel, registers a cursor, agrees on layout) plus crash-safety (dead-consumer detection via PID liveness/heartbeat, cursor reclamation when a consumer dies holding state). Also tracks further-out work: multi-NUMA, ARM64, multi-producer variants. Captured now so the knowledge isn't lost; do not start until phase-1 epic shimmy-t2d is complete.
design: Consumer registry lives in the shm header; cursor slot claim/release protocol; layout agreement against compile-time instantiation. Crash-safety: detect a dead consumer (PID liveness or heartbeat) and reclaim its cursor so Backpressure mode doesn't stall forever on a corpse. Multi-NUMA placement + cross-node coherence measurement, ARM64 (weak memory model, fence rigor, doubled test matrix), and MPMC/MPSC are separate later children of this epic.
acceptance_criteria: Not started in phase 1. When opened: (1) consumers discover + register against a running channel; (2) a dead consumer is detected and its cursor reclaimed without stalling the producer; (3) layout-mismatch on join fails loudly. Sub-scopes (multi-NUMA, ARM64, multi-producer) filed as children when this epic activates.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Scope input from shimmy-4d4 review: the discovery/handshake protocol must solve safe LATE/DYNAMIC consumer attach for Backpressure. Current in-process ring assumes a fixed consumer set registered before the stream starts (enforced by a test-only start barrier). A dynamically-attaching Backpressure consumer that starts its cursor at 0 deadlocks the producer once the ring has lapped. The handshake should let a joining consumer agree on a safe starting sequence (e.g. producer's current published seq) and atomically register its cursor so the producer's await loop accounts for it without a window. Tie this to the crash-safety work (a dead consumer's cursor and a never-arriving consumer's cursor are the same stall from the producer's side).
